channel.title: Frontion - Geopolitical News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news curated from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🚢 US Navy Disables Iranian Oil Tanker in Gulf of Oman
item.description: US military disabled an Iranian-flagged oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman on Wednesday for allegedly violating the blockade of Iranian ports, marking continued tensions even as diplomatic talks progress. [US-Iran War - AP News]

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Fires Dozens of Drones at Ukraine, Rejecting Ceasefire
item.description: Russia launched nighttime drone attacks on Ukraine despite Kyiv's unilateral ceasefire offer, with Zelenskyy calling it an "obvious spurning" of peace efforts. [Ukraine-Russia War - CBC]

item.title: 💰 Central Banks Pause Rate Cuts as Iran War Roils Markets
item.description: The Iran conflict has forced major central banks to hold interest rates steady in April, disrupting global monetary easing efforts amid inflation pressures and market volatility. [Economy - Reuters]

item.title: 🎭 Pussy Riot Protesters Swarm Russia's Venice Biennale Pavilion
item.description: Activists from Pussy Riot and FEMEN staged a protest against Russia's presence at the Venice Biennale, releasing colored smoke and swarming the pavilion in pink balaclavas. [Europe - AP News]

item.title: 🏛️ FBI Searches Virginia Senate Leader's Office in Corruption Probe
item.description: The FBI executed a search warrant at Virginia Senate Leader Louise Lucas's office as part of an ongoing corruption investigation. [US Politics - AP News]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Trump Says Iran Wants to Make a Deal
item.description: President Trump announced that Iran has expressed willingness to negotiate a comprehensive agreement with the US, signaling a potential diplomatic off-ramp after weeks of intense military confrontation. [US-Iran War - Reuters]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Seeks Comprehensive Agreement with US
item.description: Iran stated it wants a comprehensive agreement with the United States, as diplomatic efforts intensify to end the conflict and potentially reopen the Strait of Hormuz. [US-Iran War - Reuters]

item.title: 🇪🇺 US NATO Exit Would Call for EU Fiscal Creativity
item.description: The possibility of the United States leaving NATO is no longer unthinkable, and Europe would need to spend more and faster than the extra $350 billion already pledged by 2035. [Europe - Reuters]

item.title: ⚔️ Poland Uncovers Russian Professional Sabotage Cells
item.description: Poland has identified professional sabotage cells operating on Russian directives, marking an escalation in hybrid warfare tactics across Eastern Europe. [Europe - Baltimore Sun]

item.title: 🇨🇳 China's Gravity-Detecting 'Squid' Gets Closer to Spotting US Nuclear Submarines
item.description: China's advanced gravity-detecting underwater technology is advancing toward the capability to detect American nuclear submarines, raising concerns about undersea warfare balance. [Asia-Pacific - SCMP]

item.title: 🎵 Eurovision Crisis Over Israel-Gaza
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest faces mounting controversy over Israel's participation amid the Gaza conflict, with political pressure intensifying ahead of the competition. [Europe - Politico EU]

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Stance on Nuclear Talks with US
item.description: Iran's leadership position on potential nuclear negotiations with the United States as diplomatic channels remain active. [Middle East - Jerusalem Post]

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan Fires Missile in Joint Drill with US and Allies Near South China Sea
item.description: Japan's Self-Defense Forces fired a Type 88 anti-ship missile during a joint exercise with US, Australian, and Philippine forces, hitting a decommissioned Philippine Navy ship in waters facing the South China Sea. [Asia-Pacific - Reuters]

item.title: 🛢️ Russia Starts Buying Forex as Middle East War Oil Windfall Arrives
item.description: Russia has begun purchasing foreign currency as oil revenue windfalls from the Middle East conflict boost its coffers, despite sanctions pressure. [Economy - Reuters]

item.title: 🇦🇪 Gulf's Fragile Trade Lifeline Hangs on Two Eastern UAE Ports
item.description: The Gulf region's critical trade infrastructure depends heavily on two eastern UAE ports, highlighting vulnerabilities in the logistics network amid regional tensions. [Middle East - Reuters]

item.title: ⛪ Top Vatican Cardinal Criticizes Trump's Attacks on Pope Leo
item.description: A senior Vatican cardinal called Trump's criticisms of Pope Leo "strange," highlighting tensions between the US administration and the Catholic Church. [Diplomacy - Reuters]

item.title: 🌍 America Massing Troops Near Taiwan to Deter China
item.description: The United States is positioning military forces near Taiwan as part of deterrence efforts against potential Chinese aggression in the region. [Asia-Pacific - The Economist]

item.title: 🇷🇺 Putin Is Losing His Grip on Russia
item.description: Vladimir Putin's control over Russia is weakening, according to analysis examining the internal pressures and challenges facing the Russian leadership. [Europe - The Economist]

item.title: 🇧🇾 Charter97 Reports on Belarus Situation
item.description: Belarus opposition outlet Charter97 continues coverage of developments in Belarus amid ongoing political repression. [Europe - Charter97]

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU Ambassadors Back New Compensation Mechanism for Russia War Victims
item.description: EU ambassadors have supported a new mechanism to compensate victims of Russia's war in Ukraine, advancing justice efforts for those affected by the conflict. [Europe - Kyiv Independent]

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Returns Seized Ukrainian Bank Assets
item.description: Hungary has returned seized Ukrainian bank assets, marking a positive development in financial disputes between the two nations. [Europe - Kyiv Independent]

item.title: 🇺🇸 US Intelligence Links Iran Conflict to Trump Dinner Shooting Suspect
item.description: A preliminary DHS intelligence report suggests the Iran conflict may have motivated the suspect in the Trump dinner shooting incident. [US Politics - Reuters]

item.title: 🇿🇦 South African Assets Gain on Middle East Peace Hopes
item.description: South African financial assets rallied as prospects for Middle East peace improved risk sentiment in emerging markets. [Africa - Reuters]

item.title: ⛽ Gasoline Costs 52% More in US Since Iran War Began
item.description: The price of a gallon of regular gasoline in the US has climbed 31 cents in the past week and is now 52% higher than before the Iran conflict began. [Economy - AP News]

item.title: 📈 Oil Prices Sink, Stocks Rally on Strait of Hormuz Reopening Hopes
item.description: Global markets reacted positively to hopes for a diplomatic solution, with Wall Street soaring and US crude prices plunging 13% after China's foreign minister called for ceasefire. [Economy - AP News]
